description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG only) that draws a VERY dense Transformer encoder-decoder architecture diagram with TWO unrolled encoder layers and TWO unrolled decoder layers shown explicitly (not collapsed). Title 'Transformer (Encoder-Decoder, 2 Layers Unrolled)' at top in 32px bold. Two parallel vertical stacks side by side packed into the height. LEFT 'Encoder' stack, bottom to top: 'Input Embedding', 'Positional Encoding', then for EACH of Encoder Layer 1 and Encoder Layer 2 the sublayers 'Multi-Head Self-Attention', 'Add & Norm (1)', 'Feed Forward', 'Add & Norm (2)' (8 boxes across 2 layers, each layer wrapped in a labelled 'Encoder Layer k' container). RIGHT 'Decoder' stack, bottom to top: 'Output Embedding', 'Positional Encoding', then for EACH of Decoder Layer 1 and Decoder Layer 2 the sublayers 'Masked Multi-Head Attention', 'Add & Norm (1)', 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention', 'Add & Norm (2)', 'Feed Forward', 'Add & Norm (3)' (12 boxes across 2 layers, each layer wrapped in a labelled 'Decoder Layer k' container). Above the decoder stack 'Linear', 'Softmax', 'Output Probabilities'. That is ~40 labelled boxes total plus 4 layer containers. From the top of the encoder stack draw FOUR labelled cross-attention arrows (Keys-to-L1, Values-to-L1, Keys-to-L2, Values-to-L2) routing rightward into BOTH decoder layers' 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention' boxes; these long horizontal arrows must cross the gap without passing through other boxes and their 4 labels must not collide. Draw residual skip arcs on the OUTER side of each stack: 4 on the encoder's left (one per Add&Norm), 6 on the decoder's right. Each box is a labelled rectangle; the two stacks share the same baseline grid so corresponding rows align.
requirements: All ~40 sublayer boxes (10 encoder + 16 decoder + Linear + Softmax + Output Probabilities + 4 layer containers) are visible within the 1920x1080 viewport with no clipping at any edge
No two sublayer boxes overlap each other
Every box label text is fully contained inside its rectangle with no overflow past the box edges
All encoder sublayer boxes have equal vertical gaps between adjacent boxes, and likewise all decoder sublayer boxes have equal vertical gaps
The 4 layer containers each fully enclose their wrapped sublayers without any sublayer crossing the container border
All FOUR labelled cross-attention arrows run from the encoder top into the correct decoder layer 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention' boxes and none passes through any other sublayer box
The residual skip arcs (4 on encoder left, 6 on decoder right) do not pass through or overlap any sublayer box and do not overlap each other
The four cross-attention arrow labels do not overlap each other or any box
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No horizontal or vertical scrollbar appears; all content fits inside 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) for a VERY dense microservices system architecture diagram titled 'E-Commerce Platform Architecture' (32px bold, top). FIVE horizontal tiers, each a labelled band, tightly packed. Tier 1 'Client Layer': 5 boxes (Web App, Mobile App, Admin Portal, Partner API Client, IoT Device). Tier 2 'Edge Layer': 4 boxes (CDN, Load Balancer, API Gateway, WAF). Tier 3 'Services Layer': 14 boxes (Auth, User Profile, Catalog, Search, Cart, Order, Payment, Shipping, Inventory, Notification, Review, Recommendation, Pricing, Fraud). Tier 4 'Messaging Layer': 3 boxes (Kafka, RabbitMQ, EventBridge). Tier 5 'Data Layer': 8 stores (Postgres, MySQL, Redis, Elasticsearch, Cassandra, MongoDB, S3, Snowflake). Draw arrows: every client box to the Load Balancer; Load Balancer to API Gateway; API Gateway to ALL 14 services; AND these cross-service arrows that must route without passing through unrelated boxes: Order->Payment, Order->Shipping, Order->Cart, Order->Inventory, Order->Fraud, Search->Catalog, Search->Recommendation, Auth->User Profile, Payment->Fraud, Pricing->Catalog, Cart->Inventory, Recommendation->Review. Each service connects to at least one data store (14 service-to-store arrows, e.g. Auth->Redis, User Profile->Postgres, Catalog->MySQL, Search->Elasticsearch, Cart->Redis, Order->Postgres, Payment->Postgres, Shipping->Cassandra, Inventory->MongoDB, Notification->Kafka, Review->MongoDB, Recommendation->Cassandra, Pricing->Snowflake, Fraud->Snowflake) and at least 4 services emit to the Messaging Layer (Order->Kafka, Payment->RabbitMQ, Notification->EventBridge, Inventory->Kafka). That is 34 boxes and 45+ arrows.
requirements: All five tier labels (Client Layer, Edge Layer, Services Layer, Messaging Layer, Data Layer) are visible and not overlapping any box
All 5 client boxes, 4 edge boxes, 14 service boxes, 3 messaging boxes, and 8 data stores (34 elements) are visible within the viewport with no clipping
The 14 service boxes are evenly spaced horizontally within the services tier with equal gaps
Every box label is fully contained inside its box with no text overflow
No two boxes overlap each other, and tiers are vertically separated so boxes from different tiers do not overlap
Every one of the 12 inter-service arrows is present and does not pass through any box that is not its endpoint
Each of the 14 services has at least one arrow to a data store, and these service-to-data arrows do not cross through unrelated service boxes
Arrowheads do not land on top of label text
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No scrollbar appears; the whole diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a dense algorithm flowchart titled 'Quicksort with Median-of-Three + Merge Sort Fallback' (32px bold, top). Use standard shapes: rounded 'Start'/'End' terminators, rectangles for processes, diamonds for decisions, parallelograms for I/O. Flow: Start -> 'Read array A[0..n-1]' (I/O) -> 'call sort(A, 0, n-1)' -> decision 'lo < hi?'. If NO -> 'return (base case)' -> End. If YES -> decision 'hi-lo < 16?'. If YES -> 'insertionSort(A, lo, hi)' -> 'return' -> End. If NO -> decision 'depth > 2*log2(n)?'. If YES -> 'mergeSort(A, lo, hi) [fallback]' -> 'return' -> End. If NO -> 'mid=(lo+hi)/2' -> 'median-of-three(A[lo],A[mid],A[hi])' -> 'pivot = median; swap to A[hi]' -> 'i=lo-1, j=lo' -> decision 'j < hi?'. If YES -> decision 'A[j] <= pivot?'. If A[j]<=pivot YES -> 'i++; swap A[i],A[j]' -> 'j++' -> loop back to 'j < hi?'. If NO -> 'j++' -> loop back to 'j < hi?'. If 'j < hi?' is NO -> 'swap A[i+1], A[hi]' -> 'p = i+1' -> 'sort(A, lo, p-1)' -> 'sort(A, p+1, hi)' -> decision 'recursion done?'. If NO -> loop back to 'lo < hi?'. If YES -> 'copy back to A' (I/O) -> 'return' -> End. There are 22+ nodes, 6 decision diamonds, and at least 4 loop-back arrows (two short inner-loop backs to 'j < hi?', one recursion back to 'lo < hi?', plus the partition return). Every decision branch must be labelled 'Yes' or 'No' beside its outgoing arrow. Pack the chart so the inner partition loop and the recursion back-edge cross the main top-to-bottom flow.
requirements: Start/End use rounded terminators, the 6 decisions use diamonds, I/O steps use parallelograms, and processes use rectangles
All 22+ nodes are visible within the viewport with no clipping at any edge
No two nodes overlap each other
Every node label is fully contained inside its shape with no text overflow
Each decision diamond has exactly two outgoing arrows, each labelled 'Yes' or 'No'
The Yes/No edge labels do not overlap any node or any other edge label
All 4+ loop-back arrows return to the correct decision/node and do not pass through any unrelated node
Arrowheads do not land on node text and edges do not overlap each other where they can be routed apart
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No scrollbar appears; the whole flowchart fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing TWO stacked Transformer blocks in detail, titled 'Transformer Encoder Block (Internals)' (32px bold, top). Inside an outer 'N x 6' rounded container, stack these sublayers vertically from bottom to top: 'Input Embedding + Positional Encoding', 'LayerNorm (pre)', 'Q/K/V Projection (3 linears)', 'Scaled Dot-Product Attention', 'Concat Heads', 'Output Projection', 'Add (residual 1)', 'LayerNorm', 'Linear 512->2048', 'GeLU', 'Linear 2048->512', 'Add (residual 2)', 'Output'. That is 13 sublayer boxes inside the container. Draw two residual (skip) arrows on the LEFT side: one from before 'Q/K/V Projection' into 'Add (residual 1)', one from before 'Linear 512->2048' into 'Add (residual 2)'; these must route around the left without crossing any box. On the RIGHT, place a vertical stack of 3 small annotation boxes: 'h = 8 heads', 'd_model = 512', 'd_ff = 2048'. From the 'Scaled Dot-Product Attention' box, draw a small self-loop-style branch showing the softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d))V formula in a callout box on the right that connects back with a thin arrow.

The outer container fully encloses all 13 sublayers without any sublayer crossing its border

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a VERY dense finite state machine titled 'TCP Connection State Machine (Full)' (32px bold, top). States (circles, 22 total): CLOSED, LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RCVD, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT_1, FIN_WAIT_2, CLOSING, TIME_WAIT, CLOSE_WAIT, LAST_ACK, plus duplicate-half states for a simultaneous-close peer: CLOSED_B, LISTEN_B, SYN_SENT_B, SYN_RCVD_B, ESTABLISHED_B, FIN_WAIT_1_B, CLOSE_WAIT_B, LAST_ACK_B, CLOSING_B, TIME_WAIT_B, CLOSED_FINAL. Transitions with edge labels (34+): CLOSED->LISTEN 'passive open', CLOSED->SYN_SENT 'active open', LISTEN->SYN_RCVD 'recv SYN/send SYN+ACK', LISTEN->SYN_SENT 'send SYN', SYN_SENT->SYN_RCVD 'recv SYN/send ACK', SYN_SENT->ESTABLISHED 'recv SYN+ACK/send ACK', SYN_RCVD->ESTABLISHED 'recv ACK', ESTABLISHED->FIN_WAIT_1 'close/send FIN', FIN_WAIT_1->FIN_WAIT_2 'recv ACK', FIN_WAIT_1->CLOSING 'recv FIN/send ACK', FIN_WAIT_2->TIME_WAIT 'recv FIN/send ACK', CLOSING->TIME_WAIT 'recv ACK', TIME_WAIT->CLOSED_FINAL '2MSL timeout', ESTABLISHED->CLOSE_WAIT 'recv FIN/send ACK', CLOSE_WAIT->LAST_ACK 'close/send FIN', LAST_ACK->CLOSED_FINAL 'recv ACK', SYN_RCVD->FIN_WAIT_1 'close/send FIN', SYN_SENT->CLOSED 'timeout', plus a fully mirrored set for the _B peer states (CLOSED_B->LISTEN_B 'passive open', CLOSED_B->SYN_SENT_B 'active open', LISTEN_B->SYN_RCVD_B 'recv SYN/send SYN+ACK', SYN_SENT_B->ESTABLISHED_B 'recv SYN+ACK/send ACK', SYN_RCVD_B->ESTABLISHED_B 'recv ACK', ESTABLISHED_B->CLOSE_WAIT_B 'recv FIN/send ACK', CLOSE_WAIT_B->LAST_ACK_B 'close/send FIN', LAST_ACK_B->CLOSED_FINAL 'recv ACK', ESTABLISHED_B->FIN_WAIT_1_B 'close/send FIN', FIN_WAIT_1_B->CLOSING_B 'recv FIN/send ACK', CLOSING_B->TIME_WAIT_B 'recv ACK', TIME_WAIT_B->CLOSED_FINAL '2MSL timeout'), plus three cross arrows ESTABLISHED->ESTABLISHED_B 'simultaneous open', SYN_SENT->CLOSED 'timeout', SYN_SENT_B->CLOSED_B 'timeout'. Several long backward arrows must route around without passing through other states. Mark CLOSED and CLOSED_B as start states each with an incoming arrow from nowhere. At least two self-loops: ESTABLISHED->ESTABLISHED 'recv data/send ACK' and ESTABLISHED_B->ESTABLISHED_B 'recv data/send ACK'.
requirements: All 22 states are visible as circles within the viewport with no clipping
No two state circles overlap each other
Every state name is fully contained inside its circle with no text overflow
All 34+ labelled transitions are present with visible arrowheads at their targets
Both ESTABLISHED self-loops are drawn as loops returning to the same circle and are labelled
Each transition edge label is readable and does not overlap any state circle
Transition edge labels do not overlap one another
Edges do not pass through any state circle that is not their endpoint (including the long backward and cross-peer arrows)
Both CLOSED start states are marked with an incoming arrow originating from no other state, and no element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No scrollbar appears; the whole state machine fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a deep COMPLETE binary decision tree titled 'Loan Approval Decision Tree (Depth 5)' (32px bold, top), depth 5 with 32 leaves. Root (depth 0): 'Credit Score >= 700?'. Depth 1 (2 internal): 'Income >= 50k?', 'Has Collateral?'. Depth 2 (4 internal): 'Debt Ratio < 0.3?', 'Employment > 2yr?', 'Collateral >= Loan?', 'Co-signer?'. Depth 3 (8 internal): 'Loan < 100k?', 'Savings >= 6mo?', 'Debt Ratio < 0.4?', 'Bankruptcy < 7yr?', 'Credit Score >= 650?', 'LTV < 80%?', 'Co-signer Score >= 700?', 'Income >= 30k?'. Lay out as a top-down COMPLETE binary tree where every one of the 31 internal nodes branches Yes (left) / No (right); the 32 leaves are evenly spaced across the bottom row. Every edge labelled Yes or No. With 63 nodes across 6 levels in 1920px width the leaf row is extremely tight (~55px pitch), forcing precise equal spacing and tiny labels.

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a VERY dense UML sequence diagram titled 'OAuth 2.0 + Resource Access + Refresh Flow' (32px bold, top). Messages (horizontal arrows with labels), 34 in strict order: User->Browser 'open app', Browser->Client 'GET /login', Client->Browser 'redirect to authorize', Browser->AuthServer 'authorize request', AuthServer->Cache 'check session', Cache->AuthServer 'miss', AuthServer->Browser 'login prompt', User->Browser 'enter credentials', Browser->AuthServer 'POST credentials', AuthServer->UserDB 'lookup user', UserDB->AuthServer 'user record', AuthServer->AuthServer 'validate password' (SELF-CALL loop), AuthServer->AuditLog 'log login', AuthServer->Browser 'redirect with auth code', Browser->Client 'auth code', Client->AuthServer 'exchange code (nested)', AuthServer->TokenStore 'store refresh token', TokenStore->AuthServer 'ok', AuthServer->Cache 'cache access token', AuthServer->Client 'access + refresh token', Client->ResourceServer 'GET /data + token', ResourceServer->AuthServer 'introspect token', AuthServer->Cache 'lookup token', Cache->AuthServer 'valid', AuthServer->ResourceServer 'token valid', ResourceServer->Client 'protected data', Client->ResourceServer 'GET /more + token', ResourceServer->Client '401 expired', Client->AuthServer 'refresh (refresh token)', AuthServer->TokenStore 'validate refresh', TokenStore->AuthServer 'ok', AuthServer->Client 'new access token', Client->ResourceServer 'retry GET /more', ResourceServer->Client 'protected data'. Use solid arrows for requests and dashed arrows for responses. description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a VERY dense layered protocol-stack comparison titled 'OSI vs TCP/IP vs Protocols vs PDUs vs Devices vs Attacks vs Encapsulation' (32px bold, top). SEVEN vertical columns side by side, all sharing the same row heights so rows align exactly. Column 1 'OSI' has 7 stacked equal-height layers (top to bottom): Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical. Column 2 'TCP/IP' has 4 layers spanning the equivalent heights: Application (spanning OSI's top 3), Transport, Internet, Link (spanning OSI's bottom 2). Column 3 'Protocols' aligned to each OSI layer: HTTP/FTP/SMTP/DNS, TLS/SSL/JPEG, NetBIOS/RPC, TCP/UDP/SCTP, IP/ICMP/IGMP, Ethernet/PPP/ARP, RJ45/fiber/coax. Column 4 'PDU' aligned to each OSI layer: Data, Data, Data, Segment, Packet, Frame, Bit. Column 5 'Devices' aligned to each OSI layer: Gateway/Proxy, -, -, L4 Load Balancer, Router/L3 Switch, Switch/Bridge, Hub/Repeater. Column 6 'Attacks' aligned: SQLi/XSS, SSL strip, Session hijack, SYN flood, IP spoof, MAC flood, Jamming. Column 7 'Encapsulation Header' aligned: App Header, Presentation, Session, TCP Header, IP Header, MAC Header, Preamble. Draw dashed horizontal alignment guides spanning ALL seven columns at each OSI layer boundary (8 guide lines). With 7 columns in 1920px width each column is ~250px and several cells contain long multi-token text, stressing label fit and exact row alignment.

Within each column the bands are contiguous (no gaps or overlaps between adjacent bands)

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a FIVE-set Venn diagram (using the standard 5-ellipse rotationally-symmetric layout) titled 'Full-Stack & ML Skill Sets' (32px bold, top). Five congruent translucent ellipses arranged at 72-degree rotations about the center (the canonical 5-set Venn), each labelled outside the ellipse: 'Frontend', 'Backend', 'DevOps', 'Data', 'ML'. A 5-set Venn has 31 regions; place a short text label inside at least 26 of them: the 5 single-set regions ('CSS', 'SQL', 'Bash', 'Stats', 'Models'), all 10 pairwise overlaps (e.g. 'API', 'CI/CD', 'ETL', 'Dashboards', 'Monitoring', 'Edge Deploy', 'Feature Store', 'Pipelines', 'Serving', 'Viz'), at least 8 of the 10 triple-overlap regions ('Microservices', 'MLOps', 'Observability', 'DataViz', 'AutoML', 'Streaming', 'Infra-as-Code', 'Telemetry'), at least 2 of the 5 quadruple-overlap regions ('Platform', 'FullStackML'), and the central quintuple-overlap region ('Unicorn'). That is 26 region labels packed into the 31-region figure. Ellipses must be translucent so all overlaps are visible, and with 5 rotated ellipses the regions are small slivers, forcing tiny precisely-placed labels that must each sit inside the correct region without spilling.
requirements: Five congruent translucent ellipses are drawn in the canonical rotationally-symmetric 5-set arrangement with a single central region where all five overlap
The ellipses use translucent fills so that overlap regions are visibly distinct
Each ellipse has an external label (Frontend, Backend, DevOps, Data, ML) that does not overlap another ellipse's external label
The 5 single-set region labels are each inside their correct single-set-only region
All 10 specified pairwise-overlap labels are each inside the correct two-set overlap region and do not spill into an adjacent region
At least 8 triple-overlap labels are placed inside correct three-set overlap regions
The central label 'Unicorn' is inside the quintuple-overlap region and at least 2 quadruple-overlap labels are inside correct four-set regions
No region label overlaps another region label

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a dense cascade control-systems block diagram titled 'Cascade PID Feedback Control Loop' (32px bold, top). Forward path left to right: input 'r(t)' arrow -> OUTER summing junction (circle with + and - inputs) -> block 'Outer PID' -> INNER summing junction (circle with + and -) -> block 'Inner PID' -> block 'Actuator' -> block 'Plant G(s)' -> a pickoff node -> output 'y(t)'. From the pickoff after Plant, an OUTER feedback path returns leftward through 'Sensor H1(s)' to the OUTER summing junction's - input (a long lower arc). From a SECOND pickoff between Actuator and Plant, an INNER feedback path returns through 'Sensor H2(s)' to the INNER summing junction's - input (a shorter arc that must NOT cross the outer feedback arc or pass through forward blocks). Add a 'Disturbance d(t)' arrow entering a small summing junction between Actuator and Plant. Label the signals: 'r(t)', 'e1(t)' (after outer sum), 'e2(t)' (after inner sum), 'u(t)' (after inner PID), 'y(t)'. There are 3 summing junctions, 5 forward blocks, 2 sensor blocks.
requirements: All 3 summing-junction circles, the 5 forward blocks (Outer PID, Inner PID, Actuator, Plant G(s) plus disturbance junction), and the 2 sensor blocks are visible with no clipping
The outer summing junction shows + on r(t) and - on its feedback input; the inner summing junction shows + and - signs as well
Every block label is fully contained inside its block with no text overflow
The forward-path arrows (r(t)->outer sum->Outer PID->inner sum->Inner PID->Actuator->Plant->y(t)) are present and point rightward
The OUTER feedback path returns from after the plant through Sensor H1(s) to the outer junction's - input without passing through any forward-path block
The INNER feedback path returns through Sensor H2(s) to the inner junction's - input, and the inner and outer feedback arcs do not cross each other or pass through forward blocks
The Disturbance d(t) arrow enters the summing junction between Actuator and Plant and is labelled
The signal labels r(t), e1(t), e2(t), u(t), y(t) are each on the correct edge and do not overlap a block or another label
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a 2x2 strategy quadrant titled 'Effort vs Impact Prioritization' (32px bold, top). Horizontal axis labelled 'Effort' (Low on left, High on right); vertical axis labelled 'Impact' (Low at bottom, High at top). The two axes cross at center forming 4 quadrants, each with a corner title: top-left 'Quick Wins', top-right 'Major Projects', bottom-left 'Fill-Ins', bottom-right 'Thankless Tasks'. Plot 40 small labelled dots scattered across the quadrants (10 per quadrant), each with a text label beside it, many positioned CLOSE TOGETHER in clusters so labels are highly prone to overlap. Quick Wins: 'Cache layer', 'Fix typos', 'Add logging', 'Tooltip help', 'Rename vars', 'Hotkey menu', 'Dark mode', 'Copy edits', 'Favicon', 'Empty states'; Major Projects: 'Rewrite auth', 'Migrate DB', 'New billing', 'Multi-region', 'Redesign UI', 'Event sourcing', 'GraphQL API', 'Mobile app', 'SSO', 'Data lake'; Fill-Ins: 'Update docs', 'Clean dead code', 'Bump deps', 'Add unit tests', 'Lint config', 'Format code', 'Archive logs', 'Tag releases', 'Prune images', 'Rotate keys'; Thankless Tasks: 'Legacy patch', 'Manual report', 'Vendor portal', 'Compliance form', 'Data backfill', 'License audit', 'Tax export', 'GDPR review', 'Cookie banner', 'Disk cleanup'. Add a faint dashed diagonal 'priority line' from bottom-left to top-right. With 40 labelled points in a 1920x1080 frame the clusters force careful label placement to avoid collisions.
requirements: Two perpendicular axes cross at the center dividing the area into 4 quadrants
The Effort axis is labelled Low on the left and High on the right; the Impact axis is labelled Low at bottom and High at top
All 4 quadrant titles are present, each in the correct quadrant, and not overlapping the axes or any dot label
Exactly 40 dots are plotted, 10 in each quadrant
Each dot has a readable text label beside it, and no dot label overlaps another dot label
No dot or its label crosses an axis line into a neighboring quadrant
The dashed diagonal priority line runs from bottom-left to top-right and does not obscure any quadrant title
All 40 dots and labels stay within the viewport bounds
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
